verse_text: He had raised the daughter of Abihail, his father's brother, and her name was Esther; and when her parents died, he brought her up to be his wife, and she was beautiful.

links.context: The righteousness of Joseph consisted of a mercy that transcends the Law ([[HOS.6:6]]). Joseph showed this mercy by his unwillingness to expose Mary's supposed sin, even though he was obliged by the Law to do so. Her husband: The Bible calls engaged couples husband and wife before their marriage (Rachel was called the wife of Jacob before marriage by virtue of their engagement in [[GEN.29:21]]; see also [[DEU.22:23]], [[DEU.22:24]]). Thus, Joseph is called the husband of Mary, and Mary is called his wife (vv. 20, 24). In the Church, Joseph is remembered as the Betrothed, pointing out Mary's ever-virginity. (See also note on [[EST.2:7]].)
